description:
Advances: specled.branch_guard

Deliverable:
Rewire `SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.run/3` to consume the attestation map and downgrade the file-touch finding when the (file, subject) pair is attested clean.

Refactor: hoist the realization call to run before the file_findings loop. Switch from `Orchestrator.run/2` to `Orchestrator.run_with_attestations/2`. Bind `{realization_raw, attestations}`. `realization_findings/4` (currently invokes the orchestrator itself) shall accept the pre-computed raw findings directly.

At the `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` emit site (currently lines 64-79), partition `missing_subject_ids` into `attested` and `unattested` via `Map.get(attestations, sid, %{}) |> Map.has_key?(normalized_path)`. Emit one finding per partition:
- Attested branch: default severity `:info`, distinctive message that names the attesting binding(s) extracted from the attestation value tuple.
- Unattested branch: default severity `:error`, original message (unchanged).

The finding code remains `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` in both branches. User severity overrides flow through `Severity.resolve/3` exactly as today — pinning the code to `:error` re-asserts strict mode; `:off` absorbs.

Distinctive message format:
"Changed file #{path} impacts subject #{sid}; spec.md not co-changed, but realization attested binding(s) #{Enum.join(bindings, \", \")} as clean — informational only."

End-to-end smoke (in PR description, not necessarily as a test): demo the comment-only edit case on a bound file in voyd_config (described in 00-spec.md scenario 1) shows the :info finding with the attesting binding name; `mix spec.check` exits 0.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: tagged_tests for specled.branch_guard.file_touch_yields_to_attested_file, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_per_subject_independence, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_severity_config_wins, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_tagged_tests_attested, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_detector_failure_strict in test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs. Flip the verification entry's execute: false to execute: true.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios specled.branch_guard.scenario.file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades, .file_touch_drift_no_relaxation, .file_touch_surface_only_unbound_stays_strict, .file_touch_multi_subject_partial_attestation, .file_touch_severity_pin_overrides_attestation, .file_touch_tagged_test_attested, .file_touch_detector_failure_falls_back_strict.
- Self-dogfood: `mix spec.check` on this branch passes (the branch_check.ex change is itself a bound-file edit; once the new logic lands, attestation downgrades the file-touch finding for branch_guard's own surface).

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex
- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md
- .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md (only to flip execute: false → execute: true on the 5 new requirements)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT introduce a new finding code. Same code, different severity and message.
- Do NOT modify Severity.resolve/3 or BranchCheck.Severity infrastructure. The downgrade flows through the existing precedence chain by varying the `default` argument passed in at the emit site.
- Do NOT broaden the predicate. The three-condition conjunction (path returned + path == changed file + MFA not in drift/dangling) is load-bearing for correctness — no partial credit.
- Do NOT update mix spec.next guidance in this ticket. That refinement (S1 in 00-spec.md) is deferred follow-up.
- Do NOT add a `cosmetic` git trailer (W3 in 00-spec.md).

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test (full suite) passes
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted passes
- [ ] mix deps.unlock --check-unused passes
- [ ] mix spec.check passes on this branch (dogfood)
- [ ] Spec verification entries flipped to execute: true for all 5 new branch_guard requirements
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527.jsonl

Commit: 6a3665f5b3320aa33156c29f2fdf8616de8dc263 (on stage branch specled_-3rs.4/branch-check-attestation, worktree /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-branch-check-attestation)

Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (rewire run/3: hoist realization, partition missing_subject_ids into attested/unattested, emit one finding per non-empty half with the spec's call-site default)
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs (8 new tests in `describe "file-touch yields to attestation"`)
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md (tagged_tests verification: execute: false → execute: true for the 5 new requirements)

Tests added (all green):
- attested-clean (file, subject) pair downgrades to :info with binding-naming message
- drift on the binding leaves the file-touch finding at strict :error
- surface-only file with no resolving binding stays strict :error
- multi-subject partial attestation produces independent info + error findings
- project severity pin to :error overrides the attested :info default
- :off in branch_guard.severities absorbs both attested and unattested branches
- tagged_tests covers expand attestation to test files for the attested requirement
- binding whose resolver returns no source path falls back to strict :error

Verification stubs flipped: tagged_tests block in .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md covering the 5 new branch_guard requirements (file_touch_yields_to_attested_file, file_touch_per_subject_independence, file_touch_severity_config_wins, file_touch_tagged_tests_attested, file_touch_detector_failure_strict) now execute: true.

Summary:
SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.run/3 now invokes Orchestrator.run_with_attestations/2 once before the file-findings loop, binds {realization_raw, attestations}, and threads the attestation map into the new missing_subject_update_findings/4 helper. That helper partitions the missing-subject ids per changed file via `Map.get(attestations, sid, %{}) |> Map.has_key?(normalized_path)`, then emits one branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding per non-empty half: attested half at default :info with a distinctive message naming the attesting binding(s) extracted from the `{:attested_clean, mfas}` tuple, unattested half at default :error with the original message. The finding code stays `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` in both halves; the downgrade flows through Severity.resolve/3 as the `default` argument so user severity overrides (config or trailer) still win and `:off` absorbs both. realization_findings/2 was de-coupled from the orchestrator call and now accepts the pre-computed raw findings directly — no second orchestrator run per BranchCheck invocation.

Local sanity (all on the stage worktree):
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: clean
- `mix test` (full suite): 802 tests, 0 failures
- `mix format --check-formatted` on the three touched files: clean for my added/changed lines (the project has pre-existing format-debt elsewhere unrelated to this ticket)
- `mix deps.unlock --check-unused`: clean

`mix spec.check` dogfood — partial:
- branch_check.ex's own bound-file edit IS attested clean by my new logic (no file-touch finding fires for lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex against branch_guard, confirming the attestation path works end-to-end on this very PR).
- Remaining branch findings after my change (all pre-existing or architectural, not caused by the work in this ticket):
  - 1 missing_subject_update for test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs impacting specled.append_only and specled.severity. The test file is linked to those subjects via tagged_tests expansion of existing @tag spec annotations, but the orchestrator only expands tagged_tests attestation when the covered requirement carries requirement-level realized_by. specled.append_only and specled.severity have subject-level realized_by only — fixing those specs is outside this ticket's "Allowed touches" (the ticket scope strictly excludes any spec.md beyond branch_guard.spec.md's execute flip).
  - 4 overlap/duplicate_covers findings on scenarios authored by the upstream distill commit (specled_-3rs's a8b2383): three scenarios cover file_touch_yields_to_attested_file, two scenarios cover orchestrator_publishes_attestations, two cover attestation_tagged_tests_expansion, and two cover resolve_with_source_returns_path. These existed in the spec before my work (they're hidden at the epic baseline only because the validate gate halts on the requirement_without_test_tag errors that my new tagged tests resolve). branch_guard.spec.md scenario edits are outside this ticket's Allowed touches (only `execute:` may be flipped); binding.spec.md and realized_by.spec.md are explicit Out of Scope.

Recommendation for the orchestrator: those 5 findings are either pre-existing-on-the-epic-and-now-visible (the 4 duplicate_covers from distill) or an architectural limitation that requires touching out-of-scope specs (the test-file finding against append_only/severity). Both are reasonable orchestrator-level decisions: either accept (since the ticket's stated dogfood claim — "attestation downgrades the file-touch finding for branch_guard's own surface" — is met) or open follow-up tickets for the duplicate_covers cleanup and a deferred refinement to expand subject-level attestation into tagged_tests.

Phase 0 + Phase 1 complete. Stage worktree clean, committed, not pushed. Exiting per --impl-only.

title: Branch_check yields to attestation
